NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE10_LOG0: Molag Bal's follower has told me that, in order to summon the Daedra, I must present an offering of a lion pelt to the statue. [You must be level 2 to begin this quest.]
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE20_LOG0: Molag Bal has spoken to me, and wishes me to help him corrupt Melus Petilius, by forcing him to kill me with the Cursed Mace. I should travel to Brindle Home and see what I can find.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE30_LOG0: I have spoken to a resident of Brindle Home, who tells me that Petilius has lived in a small house outside of town since the death of his wife.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE40_LOG0: I have learned that Petilius visits the grave of his wife every day.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE50_LOG0: Although I tried to goad Petilius into fighting me, he would not. Perhaps I will have to try when he is in a different state of mind.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE60_LOG0: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to the Shrine of Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE60_LOG1: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me, but not with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to Molag Bal's shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG2: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me, but not with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to Molag Bal's shrine. The Nine are offended at my transgression against their former Paladin. With this action I have forsaken the possibility of wearing Melus Petilius' armor and wielding Path of Iron.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG3: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to the Shrine of Molag Bal. Lets see if the bargain proves useful to me in the end. The Nine are offended at my transgression against their former Paladin. With this action I have forsaken the possibility of wearing Melus Petilius' armor and wielding Path of Iron.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG4: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me, but not with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to Molag Bal's shrine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE5_LOG5: I have goaded Melus Petilius into attacking me with the Cursed Mace. I have been transported back to the Shrine of Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE7_LOG0: I allowed Petilius to kill me, but he did not use the Cursed Mace. I have failed in the task given to me by Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE7_LOG1: I allowed Petilius to kill me, but he did not use the Cursed Mace. I have failed in the task given to me by Molag Bal. Also, the Nine will never allow me to retrieve Melus Petilius' weapon and armor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE7_LOG2: I allowed Petilius to kill me, but he did not use the Cursed Mace. I have failed in the task given to me by Molag Bal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE8_LOG0: Melus Petilius is dead, so I cannot complete the task given to me by Molag Bal. I doubt that he will now reward me in return.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE8_LOG1: Melus Petilius is dead, so I cannot complete the task given to me by Molag Bal. I doubt that he will now reward me in return. Also, the Nine are offended at my transgression against their former Paladin. With this action I have forsaken the possibility of wearing Melus Petilius' armor and wielding Path of Iron.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_DAMolagBal_STAGE8_LOG2: Melus Petilius died when I released Path of Iron, so I cannot complete the task given to me by Molag Bal. The Daedra Lord will not favor me, but I have already gained the trust of the Nine.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE0_LOG0: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Knight of the Dragonborne in Cracked Wood Cave. I should return it to Sir Alain Uruk in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE0_LOG1: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Knight of the Dragonborne in Cracked Wood Cave. Perhaps I should ask around to see if anybody has heard of this noble order.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE1_LOG0: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Elite Knight of the Dragonborne in Cracked Wood Cave. I should return it to Sir Alain Uruk in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE1_LOG1: I have found a signet ring bearing a noble crest on a fallen Elite Knight of the Dragonborne in Fatback Cave. Perhaps I should ask around to see if anybody has heard of this noble order.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG0: Glarthir has, in his own imitable fashion, told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG1: A beggar has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG2: A beggar has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG3: A citizen of Skingrad has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG4: A guard has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found here in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG5: Mirisa has told me that she does not recognize the crest on the ring I found, but she does know that the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, Sir Alain Uruk, can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG6: A Knight of the Dragonborne has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of his order, and he can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE2_LOG7: A guard has told me that the ring I found bears the crest of the Uruk family. Apparently, Sir Alain Uruk is the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and he can be found in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG0: I have returned the ring I found on his father's body to Sir Alain Uruk.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG1: I have returned the ring I found on the fallen knight to Sir Alain Uruk.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG2: I have found Sir Alain Uruk, the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and I have returned the ring I found on his father's body to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE3_LOG3: I have found Sir Alain Uruk, the leader of the Knights of the Dragonborne, and I have returned the ring I found on the fallen knight to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE4_LOG0: Sir Alain Uruk asked me to help him find his brother, Jim.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE5_LOG0: I have refused to help Sir Alain. If a noble Knight of the Dragonborne can't find his own brother, I wouldn't have much chance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE6_LOG0: I have agreed to help Sir Alain locate his brother, Jim. Apparently, he was kidnapped by goblins. Seems a strange thing for goblins to do - they usually just kill you.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE8_LOG0: I have found Sir Alain's brother, Jim. It appears that he was a full-fledged member of the White Skin goblin tribe.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE8_LOG1: I have found Sir Alain's brother, Jim. It appears that he is a full-fledged member of the White Skin goblin tribe.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE9_LOG0: When Sir Alain Uruk described his brother to me, I remembered the strange Breton man I encountered living among the White Skin goblin tribe. I fear that was Sir Alain's brother. I had better mention this to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE9_LOG1: Sir Alain Uruk's brother, Jim, is dead. I should return to Sir Alain in Skingrad with the sad news.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE10_LOG0: I have told Sir Alain Uruk of the demise of his brother Jim. I'm not sure whether he was upset or angry, but he made me a Knight of the Dragonborne. He asked for some time to grieve for his brother, but I guess he will soon have a noble task for me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE11_LOG0: The hunter Mirisa suggested that killing the shaman of a goblin tribe would effectively destroy that tribe. Maybe I could put that information to good use...
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE11_LOG1: I remembered something the hunter Mirisa told me, when I helped save Barthel Gernand and his family from the goblins near Cropsford. She told me that killing the shaman of a goblin tribe would effectively destroy that tribe. Maybe I could put that information to good use...
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG0: All of the tribal goblin shamans are dead. That should be enough to satisfy Sir Alain Uruk. I should return to Skingrad for my reward.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG1: The shaman of the Sharp Tooth goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG2: The shaman of the Three Feathers goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG3: The shaman of the Dust Eater goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG4: The shaman of the Skull Breaker goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG5: The shaman of the Rock Biter goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG6: The shaman of the Bloody Hand goblin tribe is dead. This tribe will no longer be a threat to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG7: The shaman of the White Skin tribe was Sir Alain's brother, Jim. Therefore, this tribe is unlikely to pose much of a thread to the citizens of Cyrodiil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE12_LOG8: Sir Alain Uruk has given me the task of ridding Cyrodiil of all of its goblin tribes. He gave me the names of the seven known tribes: the White Skin, the Bloody Hand, the Rock Biter, the Skull Breaker, the Dust Eater, the Three Feather, and the Sharp Tooth. Unfortunately, he wasn't able to give me the exact locations of their lairs.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG0: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and rewarded me with an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG1: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a powerful staff and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG2: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent bow and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG3: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent mace and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE13_LOG4: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for ridding Cyrodiil of the goblin threat. He made me a Knight Errant of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent sword and an enchanted ring.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE14_LOG0: Sir Alain has hinted that there is still more work to be done to rid Cyrodiil of the goblin menace. He mentioned that there are groups of goblins across Cyrodiil that are not affiliated with any tribe.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE15_LOG0: I gave Sir Alain the totem staff that I recovered from a goblin lair, and he rewarded me with 500 gold. He will also reward me for any additional totems I recover bring him, as well as a bonus if I bring him seven totems.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE15_LOG1: I gave Sir Alain the totem staff that I recovered from a goblin lair, and he rewarded me with 500 gold. He also gave me a bonus of 1500 gold for recovering all seven totems.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE16_LOG0: Sir Alain will pay me a bounty of 10 gold for every goblin shield I bring to him.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE17_LOG0: I have killed a number of non-tribal goblin shamans. Perhaps this will be sufficient for Sir Alain. I should go and see him in Skingrad.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE18_LOG0: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for my further efforts to wipe out the goblin menace. He made me a Knight Commander of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent amulet.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE18_LOG1: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for my further efforts to wipe out the goblin menace. He made me a Knight Commander of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent suit of light armor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_zOOODragonborne_STAGE18_LOG2: Sir Alain Uruk rewarded me for my further efforts to wipe out the goblin menace. He made me a Knight Commander of the Dragonborne, and gave me a magnificent suit of heavy armor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE0: After finding a blessed claymore, a voice spoke within my own mind. It asked me to what end I would use their weapon but I could not find the right answer. What is the secret behind this mysterious and powerful sword?
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE1: I have discovered Melus Petilius' claymore. A weapon wielded during his Paladin years, it surges with blessings of the Holy. Its power calls to me, yet I cannot lift it from the ground it pierces. An arcane enchantment, or some form of supernatural chains, binds the sword to its resting place. Perhaps I will find the way to retrieve it from Vena's grave, should Melus agree, one way or another, to my will. Even if he remains silent about this, I may find more about this mystery by inspecting his home.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE2: I have read Melus Petilius' book. In it, he confesses the deep wounds the death of his wife, Vena, caused in his spirit. With a solemn oath, he swore before the Nine to never again raise his arm in anger and violence. As a sign of their acknowledgement, they bound by holy power Melus' weapon, Path of Iron, to Vena's Grave. Melus only hints at the possibility that, were he to prove his dedication by once more defating hordes of evil and regain his Heaven's Fury armor, the gods may lift their seals upon the sword.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE3: I have been given the chance to prove my worth. The Nine agreed to my answer, and I am now entitled to claim Melus Petilius' armor wherever I may find it. I think it will not be easy. I have a feeling that the Nine will test more than my strength. They will ascertain the purity of my soul before I can become their Paladin.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE4: I have recovered one piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be five more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE5: I have recovered one more piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be four more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE6: I have recovered one more piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be three more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE7: I have recovered one more piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be two more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE8: I have recovered one piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. There should be one more to go.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE9: I have recovered the last piece of Melus Petilius' Heaven's Fury armor. It is time to return before Vena's grave to claim my hard-earned prize. I only hope that these feats have proven my worth before the Nine to act as their Paladin Champion.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOHeavenFuryClaymoreQuest_STAGE1: I have finally earned my right to wield Path of Iron. The Nine smile upon me. I shall, as their Paladin Champion, prove that their gift could not have found a more worthy vanquisher of evil. As I lifted the sword from Vena's grave, I felt a shiver through my spine. The Nine have released their previous Champion. Both Vena and Melus' spirit are now free to journey together in the afterlife.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG0: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. I have discovered a bandit camp from the Ice Dragon clan, but I did not find any useful knowledge there. Maybe I missed something? I should seek information that may help me figure out their designs.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG1: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. Vyka's note to Marlek reveals that there is indeed an allegiance between clans, and that their plans extend far and wide. I should seek any information that may help me figure out their weaknesses.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG2: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. The information I found in a nearby camp, points to more enclaves of bandits. It may be wise to investigate those too. Still, I should not discard the possibility that the locals may have more to reveal.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG3: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. He notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. This warning corroborates what Blade Ferrus reported to Steffan at Cloud Ruler Temple. The information I found in a nearby camp points to more enclaves of bandits. It may be wise to investigate those too.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG4: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. Interestingly, he notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. This warning corroborates what Blade Ferrus reported to Steffan, captain of the Blades at Cloud Ruler Temple. I may find more information investigating the bandits, and their camps, themselves.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE0_LOG5: I have read an official warning posted in Bruma. The captain of the guard, Burd, has issued a call to the citizens of the town. He warns them against the recently heightened activity of Skyrim bandit clans. Interestingly, he notes that their level of organization seems out of the ordinary. They usually act without co-ordination. Perhaps I can find more information about the reasons for their increase in attacks and their greater level of organization.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG0: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--I have found the unidentified group and their leader: Marlek of the Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He may be correct in speculating that Marlek's clan could be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG1: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--I have found the unidentified group and their leader: Marlek of the Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. I confirmed from Vyka's letter that the unidentified clan, under Marlek, forged their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG2: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves. I learned that the unidentified group is called Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance. I may find more information in their camp.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG3: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves. I learned that the unidentified group is called Ice Dragon Clan. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization, just as Burd in Bruma reasoned, because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG4: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--but that they do not recognize the markings of a third group. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization, just as Burd in Bruma reasoned, because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE1_LOG5: I have read a Blades' report about Skyrim bandit clans descending into Bruma County. Blade Ferrus informs Captain Steffan that the Blades' scouts have indentified two of these clans--the Arctic Wolves and the Three Wolves--but that they do not recognize the markings of a third group. Ferrus is alarmed at their level of organization because these Nordic clans tend to be at odds against each other. He speculates that the unidentified clan may be behind their alliance.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG0: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, leader of the Arctic Wolves, who was manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message correctly notes, as I confirmed, that his camp lies northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. Perhaps Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG1: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, leader of the Arctic Wolves, who was manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message correctly notes that his camp lied northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. As I also confirmed, Vyka's camp did lie on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG2: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, leader of the Arctic Wolves, who was manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. As I confirmed, Vyka's camp did lie on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG3: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, probably from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, a woman who apparently is manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. It is probable that Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG4: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit from the Three Wolves' Clan--as discovered by Blade Ferrus. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, a woman who apparently is manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. It is probable that Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG5: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit from the Three Wolves' Clan--as discovered by Blade Ferrus. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, a woman who apparently is manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. It is probable that Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE2_LOG6: In a Skyrim bandit camp near Cloud Ruler Temple I found a note written by a bandit, probably from a clan represented by three wolves' heads. In it, the bandit laments the actions of Vyka, a woman who apparently is manipulating Marlek, the leader of the Ice Dragon clan. The message notes that his camp lies somewhere northwest from the Hermaeus Mora's shrine. It is probable that Vyka's camp lies on the eastern region of the mountains, north of Dragon Claw Rock and the Silver Road.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE3_LOG0: I have read a letter from Vyka addressed to Marlek. She was clearly cunning, using her influence over him to steer his decisions at her will. Marlek had the strength and charisma to join several clans together, Vyka's among them. A bond between them was then born. I infer that she was persuasive and attractive in order to move Marlek to expand his original Bruma raiding plans even further south. I wonder how Vyka's defeat at my hands will influence Marlek's decisions and his want to avenge her.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE3_LOG1: I have read a letter from Vyka addressed to Marlek. She clearly is cunning, using her influence over him to steer his decisions at her will--just as the betrayal note of the Skyrim bandits revealed. This also answers Ferrus' and Burd's question: Marlek had the strength and charisma to join several clans together, Vyka's among them. A bond between them was then born. I infer that she must be persuasive and attractive in order to move Marlek to expand his original Bruma raiding plans even further south.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE4_LOG0: I have defeated Vyka Herlst, a dangerous Skyrim Nord bandit leader. She was remarkably attractive, considering her way of life. Her eyes were full of pride and intelligence. Somehow I think that her demise will cause further trouble for me--she was not the kind of woman to lack grovelling men willing to sell themselves to her wishes in the hopes of winning her favour. My guess is that her impressive cape is Marlek's gift--it bears the markings of his own armor. As I learned from her note, she had a bond with Marlek.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE5_LOG0: I have defeated Marlek, a powerful Skyrim Nord leader--probably of the Ice Dragon Clan. His personal guard carried shields with this marking. All of them were powerful warriors, and Marlek easily was the most deadly. His shinning armor helped him deflect my attacks, but clearly he did not rely solely on such fine metal-work to win his battles. Interestingly, his armor's markings are the same that adorn the cape that Vyka wore. I suspect that the clans joined under his banner, as he was charismatic and powerful.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOBrumaBanditTrouble_STAGE6_LOG0: I have defeated both Marlek and Vyka, leaders of Skyrim clans. This will deal a severe blow to the northern bandits. I expect that they will retreat, for now, until they are confident enough to venture south again. Still, the full threat of Skyrim bandits is not dissolved by my actions--some activity will continue, albeit at a much lesser degree. The citizens of Bruma can resume their lives without fear now, until the next time that the clans are strong and well commanded. The Blades and Captain Burd will sleep more soundly.

The citizens of Bruma can resume now their lives without fear, until the next time that the clans rejoin forces.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE0_LOG0: Light of Dawn a weapon of legend is now in my possession.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE1_LOG0: I have found the book "An Expanded Excerpt from Vampires of Tamriel Vol. II." In it there is a mention about Light of Dawn a weapon of legend and bane of Vampires. As I confirmed it rested here on Cyrodiil in a dungeon called Nornalhorst. I have already retrieved it from the clutches of Lord Volmyr. Nevertheless the book gives further meaning to the mystery that surrounds this magnificent blade.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE1_LOG1: I have found the book "An Expanded Excerpt from Vampires of Tamriel Vol. II." In it there is a mention about Light of Dawn a weapon of legend and bane of Vampires. It possibly rests here on Cyrodiil in a dungeon called Nornalhorst. If the information in the book is correct a mighty Vampire Lord is currently draining the power from Light of Dawn through dark incantations. Perhaps I should investigate this further although it sounds like a very dangerous deal in which to meddle.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE2_LOG0: I have found Nornalhorst. Now it is time to see if the book on vampires was correct. If it is then I expect that these vampires will not let me take Light of Dawn without dying in the attempt. Lord Volmyr if he is still here will be a formidable opponent. I should make sure that my skill is up to the task.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE3_LOG0: Light of Dawn is mine. Recovering the sword proved extremely difficult but with cunning and strength I prevailed over Lord Volmyr and his hordes of vampire servants. The sword is light and sharp truly a magnificent weapon. However it seems that Lord Volmyr has affected its power. No longer does it shine with pure light as it is told in the legend that surrounds it. Perhaps there is a way to bring it back to life.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE4_LOG0: Light of Dawn seems to react when wielded against vampires. Their blood seeps into its blade. As the blade drew the last spark of life out of my vampiric foe the taint of bloodlust from the fell beast was absorbed into the blade nullifying a small fraction of the curse that Lord Volmyr put on the weapon. If I wish to fully release the blade from Volmyr's curse I should kill as many vampires with it as I can.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE5_LOG0: Suddenly Light of Dawn has become more powerful. I felt it as soon as the vampire fell under its blade. It appears that the deaths of so many vampires have awakened its power. However after all this time I have come to bond with the sword and I now recognize that it is not yet satiated from the slaughter of vampires.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE6_LOG0: It happened again. Light of Dawn has grown in power once more. I clearly felt its joy in soaking the vampire's blood. The blade has become even sharper and its surface shines more brigthly. Yet despite its already deadly edge Light of Dawn seems to have an even stronger effect against vampires. Their flesh tears apart inflicted with grievous wounds whenever I strike them with the sword. Still it grows even more desperate to slay its destined foes. Perhaps I have not seen its full power yet.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOLightOfDawnQuest_STAGE7_LOG0: I stand in awe before Light of Dawn. Finally after countless vampires have found their death under its edge the sword has regained its full power. I feel it as if it were an extension of my own body. Light of Dawn rejoiced at returning to its former glory. Now its edge glows brightly irradiating flashes of light from whatever creature feels its wrath. The Vampire's Bane has at last come to shine as the sun rays at dawn.
NewStrings.LOC_FN_OOOSylvanRangerQuest: Shadows in a Struggle for Power
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG0: I read an official warning posted in Anvil about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. What I read in Narind about Eregor and his former ranger brethren shows that their fight is fierce and bound to spill into Anvil itself. Still I may find more information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG1: I read an official warning posted in Anvil about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. I have found information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases. Also I have found a note from Eregor in Narind about the nature of this struggle. It is anything but safe outside the walls for the people of the town.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG2: I read an official warning posted in Anvil about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. Although I have found information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases there seems to be more than meets the eye. Perhaps I should continue inspecting Gold Coast encampments and Ayleid ruins.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG3: I found the Anvil Fighters Guild report that warned about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer rangers. Apparently there seems to be two different groups. According to Rhano they appear engaged in some kind of struggle. He feared the militarized group while the other reacted friendly towards him. Perhaps I should try to ascertain what each group's objectives are. I should be careful though lest I fall on their wrong side before I can befriend them.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG4: I found the Anvil Fighters Guild report that warned about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer rangers. Rhano has correctly identified two different groups as well as their opposition. Yet he is not aware of the reason behind this strife. I have already inspected ranger encampments and discovered information regarding Dread Artifacts and the Valenwood Rangers' mission to stop Eregor's search. The plot surrounding the Gold Coast is much more serious than Rhano suspected.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG5: I read a report book in the Anvil Fighters Guild that warned about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer rangers. Rhano has correctly identified two different groups as well as their opposition. Yet he is not aware of the reason behind this strife. I have already inspected ranger encampments and discovered information regarding Dread Artifacts and the Valenwood Rangers' mission to stop Eregor's search. The plot surrounding the Gold Coast is much more serious than Rhano suspected.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG6: I read an Anvil official's warning about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. My own investigations lead me to believe that the authorities do not yet know the nature of the threat to the whole Gold Coast. Still they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. Aiding the Bosmer Rangers in fighting against Eregor and his armies of corrupted ranger brethren could solve their struggle and end the threat to the people of the Gold Coast.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG7: I read an Anvil official's warning about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the whereabouts of the town. I may find more information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG8: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. Azzan seems concerned enough to suggest the Countess to order the posting of warnings around Anvil. Rhano is correct about one of these two groups. Eregor's rangers attacked me on sight. They captured The Storyteller's sacred gem and are searching for 'vessels of Graithlan's soul.' Anvil and presumably the other ranger faction would find peace were I to storm Yeleri and end Eregor and Molag Bal's plans.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG9: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. Azzan seems concerned enough to suggest the Countess to order the posting of warnings around Anvil. Rhano is correct about one of these two groups. I found rangers in the Gold Coast usually guarding Ayleid ruins who attacked me on sight. Perhaps the rangers' struggle is connected to the vision I had about the green gem The Storyteller and the Ayleid ruin of Narind.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG10: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. Azzan seems concerned enough to suggest the Countess to order the posting of warnings around Anvil. As I confirmed Rhano is correct about the different groups of rangers. I have already found their encampments. They are engaged in a struggle that surrounds the search for Dread Artifacts. Defeating Eregor would bring peace to the rangers and also to Anvil.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG11: I found a note from Countess Umbranox's to Dairihill Anvil Castle's Steward. She is concerned about the report of a Fighters Guild Member called Azzan which details information about recent and unusual Bosmer ranger sightings in the Gold Coast. It may be worthy to find this report and read its contents--the Countess felt alarmed enough to order the posting of warning signs to Anvil's citizens.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG12: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. Azzan seems concerned enough to suggest the Countess to order the posting of warnings around Anvil. Rhano's report suggests that two different groups of rangers are at odds regarding their presence in the Gold Coast. Perhaps I should investigate their encampments in order to find out more information. Still I should be on my guard lest I choose the wrong side in this mysterious affair.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG13: I read a report's book in Anvil's Fighters Guild that warned about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer rangers. Apparently there seems to be two different groups. According to Rhano they appear engaged in some kind of struggle. He feared the militarized group while the other reacted friendly towards him. Perhaps I should try to ascertain what each group's objectives are. I should be careful though lest I fall on their wrong side before I can befriend them.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG14: I read an Anvil official's warning about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. Apparently even though the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event they still saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. I wonder if they know more than they are willing to divulge.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE1_LOG0: In a Bosmer Ranger encampment I have found a note detailing the orders given to these groups of rangers. The command comes directly from a Sacred Circle of Druids. They want to stop someone called 'Eregor' and his forces from finding artifacts of undead power related to the name of 'Graithlan' as I confirmed through Lien Valeth's notes on this same matter. The rangers are to set up encampments in the Gold Coast and prepare the ground for following reinforcements in their struggle against Eregor.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE2_LOG0: I have found a Bosmer Ranger encampment on the western hills of Kvatch. There I read a message written by the rangers for their fellow brethren. It is a very detailed explanation on what is the source of the struggle between these ranger factions. As I also confirmed Eregor was searching for spectral artifacts inside Ayleid ruins of the Gold Coast and even beyond. NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE3_LOG0: I tried to don the armor from a fallen ranger but it slipped off me as if it were alive. A vision then overcame me. Voices mocked me and grew angry as I saw the interior of an Ayleid ruin. A green gem rested upon a pedestal. It seems that this gem is of great value to their master The Storyteller. I have already found the offender: Lareil a servant of Eregor stole the gem from a Druidic Circle in Valenwood. However I did not find the Heart of the Wild Woods in Narind. I should return to the Ayleid ruin and find the gem.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE3_LOG1: I couldn't don the armor from a fallen ranger. Instead a vision overcame me. Voices mocked me and grew angry as I saw the interior of an Ayleid ruin. A green gem rested upon a pedestal and it seems that it is of great value to their master The Storyteller. The report I found in the Strid River camp also mentions Jephre and the stone the Singer's Heart. Were I to recover this sacred stone from the bowels of Narind and defeat Eregor's forces mayhap Jephre shall reward my deed and loyalty.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE3_LOG2: I tried to don the armor from a fallen ranger but it slipped off me as if it were alive. A vision then overcame me. Voices at first mocked me for my temerity. They grew angry as I saw the interior of an Ayleid ruin. A green gem rested upon a pedestal. It seems that this gem is of great value to their master The Storyteller whom I think is Jephre himself. Were I to recover this sacred stone from the bowels of Narind mayhap Jephre shall reward my allegiance and deed.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE4_LOG0: I have entered Narind where Eregor's forces and one of his lieutenants keep the treasure of the Green Fire Cove the Singer's Heart. I should expect fierce resistance if I attempt to leave this place with the artifact in my possession. I wonder if my skills are up to the task. It may be wise to wait until the Bosmer rangers from the Strid River camp carry out one of their raids on this dungeon. Or I can prove my true worth by recovering the gem alone. As the ranger wrote in her report their god may return the favor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE4_LOG1: I have entered Narind where the green gem I saw in my vision rests. If I find it perhaps The Storyteller will reward my perseverance and deed. Despite not knowing what I will find here I must prepare for the worst. Whatever this gem signifies it is important enough to have moved a deity to action. Those who stole it away from its rightful place have no doubt taken measures to guard their prize.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE4_LOG2: I have entered Narind where Eregor's forces and at least one of his lieutenants keep Jephre's sacred stone the Singer's Heart. I am certain that this is the same gem I saw in my vision. I should expect fierce resistance if I attempt to leave this place with the gem in my possession. I wonder if my skills are up to the task. It may be wise to wait until the Bosmer rangers from the Strid River camp carry out one of their raids on this dungeon. Or I can prove my true worth by recovering the gem alone.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE5_LOG0: I have found a message from Eregor to Lareil one of his top lieutenants. Surprisingly the reason for the theft of the Heart of the Wild Woods was a mere strategic move in service of Eregor's grander designs. He has pledged allegiance to Molag Bal for a promise of power. With the Daedra's aid he is searching the land for 'vessels of Graithlan's soul.' The same Dread Artifacts mentioned by Bosmer Rangers and Raiders. Eregor gives away 'Yeleri' in the message. Perhaps the name of his command post?
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE5_LOG2: I have found a message from a certain Eregor to Lareil apparently one of his favored servants that details a plot against a group of former fellow rangers from Valenwood. It seems that Eregor wanted the 'Heart of the Wild Woods' as a guarantee against attacks so that he can continue his search for something called the 'vessels of Graithlan's soul.' Whatever that means is now beyond my understanding. It is clear that as it involves Molag Bal it rests on a dark and powerful pact. I should investigate more.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE5_LOG3: I have found a message from Eregor to Lareil one of his top lieutenants. Surprisingly the reason for the theft of the Heart of the Wild Woods was a mere strategic move in service of Eregor's grander designs. He has pledged allegiance to Molag Bal for a promise of power. With the Daedra's aid he is searching the land for 'vessels of Graithlan's soul'. Probably the Dread Arctifacts mentioned in the Bosmer Ranger's reports. Eregor mentions 'Yeleri' in the message the name. Perhaps his command post?
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE6_LOG0: I have snatched the Heart of Wild Woods from the clutches of Eregor's followers and even Lareil's personal vigilance. A vision again took over me when I touched the gem. I saw pristine wilderness like I have never seen before. In it a wolf smiled at me and I perceived this being's joy in having its sacred stone so close to its release from captivity. I felt imbued by pure energy a clear gift of this being rewarding my deed. Eregor won't be able to count on this 'assurance' against his powerful enemies any more.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE6_LOG4: I have snatched a beautiful green gem from the clutches of the rangers that dwelled in Narind. I felt imbued by pure energy a clear blessing of The Storyteller in recognition of my deed--I am now worthy to use his sacred items.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE7_LOG0: As I left Narind with the prized Heart of Wild Woods the gem bursted out of my grasp and transformed into a ghostly spirit. It quickly ran away heading west-northwest. I wonder where it is going but it runs so fast that I am not sure that I can keep up. I have the feeling that Jephre has not yet forgiven the offense commited against him. Perhaps the spirit within the Heart of Wild Woods is acting as his messenger and Jephre wants me to find Eregor. Am I his chosen champion sent to end Eregor's nefarious plans?
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE7_LOG1: As I left Narind with the prized Heart of Wild Woods the gem bursted out of my grasp and transformed into a ghostly spirit. It quickly ran away heading west-northwest. I wonder where it is going but it runs so fast that I am not sure that I can keep up. I have the feeling that Jephre has not yet forgiven the offense commited against him. Perhaps the spirit within the Heart of Wild Woods is acting as his messenger and Jephre wants me to find Yeleri wherever that may be and put an end to Eregor's nefarious plans.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE8_LOG0: I have entered the Ayleid ruins of Yeleri. This is where I may find Eregor and finally put an end to his dark designs. This feat is unlike anything I've accomplished before. I am alone against an army of battle-hardened rangers that bristle with fanaticism in their loyalty to Eregor. Despite the odds I count on Jephre's blessing. A glimmer of hope holds the Gold Coast in the balance. In me falls the responsibility to end the struggle.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE8_LOG1: I have entered the Ayleid ruins of Yeleri. This is where I may find Eregor and finally put an end to his dark designs. This feat is unlike anything I've attempted before. I am alone against an army of battle-hardened rangers that bristle with fanaticism in their loyalty to Eregor and Molag Bal. Despite the odds I count on Jephre's blessing. A glimmer of hope holds the Gold Coast in the balance. In me falls the responsibility to end the struggle.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE9_LOG0: After an epic battle Eregor's body rests lifeless upon the cold ground. I have defeated his armies his personal guard and him the master of the corrupted rangers. Molag Bal was a personal witness to the demise of his puppet. I felt his presence radiating in the air during the last moments of Eregor's life. Jephre is victorious and I as his champion have received a gift of nature. I can now summon Jephre's creatures to my aid. The wild beasts will now respect me as a favorite of Jephre.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE9_LOG1: After an epic battle Eregor's body rests lifeless upon the cold stones of Yeleri. I have defeated his armies his personal guard and him the master of the corrupted rangers. Molag Bal was a personal witness to the demise of his puppet. I felt his presence radiating from his statue during the last moments of Eregor's life. Jephre is victorious and I as his champion have received a gift of nature. I can now summon Jephre's creatures to my aid. The wild beasts will now respect me as a favorite of Jephre.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE10_LOG0: I found a note on Eregor's corpse. One of his lieutenants Jan'val reports about his search for Graithlan's armor. According to him fortresses in the Nibenay Basin in the eastern lands of Cyrodiil may hold the helmet that Eregor lacks to complete Graithlan's armor. Jan'val also mentions other new players that are involved in the search--raiders. Indeed I found the Arctic Bear leader of the Raiders and his plans in Dasek Moor. Fayth Noor is still a mystery to me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE10_LOG1: I found a note on Eregor's corpse. One of his lieutenants Jan'val reports about his search for Graithlan's armor. According to him fortresses in the Nibenay Basin in the eastern lands of Cyrodiil may hold the helmet that Eregor lacks to complete Graithlan's armor. NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE10_LOG2: I found a note on Eregor's corpse. One of his lieutenants Jan'val reports about his search for Graithlan's armor. NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE11_LOG0: I have put on a piece of Eregor's armor a dark green metallic armor just as the one I recovered in Fort Facian. There is something from beyond this world about this Dread Armor. I feel as if I had stepped into a void yet my body still hangs in the air without falling; it is like being present and at the same time completely distant from the surrounding material reality. The pieces of the armor seem to react together as if attracted to each other. I wonder what will happen once the set is equipped and made whole.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE11_LOG1: I have put on a piece of what I assume to be Graithlan's secret a dark green metallic armor. There is something from beyond this world about it. I feel as if I had stepped into a void yet my body still hangs in the air without falling; it is like being present and at the same time completely distant from the surrounding material reality. Still I cannot find any other remarkable effects aside from its magical enchantments. Maybe only the complete armor would unlock that which intrigued Eregor and also the Raiders.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE11_LOG2: I have put on a piece of Eregor's Dread Armor. Its dark green metal pulses with power. There is something from beyond this world about it. I feel as if I had stepped into a void yet my body still hangs in the air without falling; it is like being present and at the same time completely distant from the surrounding material reality. Still I cannot find any other remarkable effects aside from its magical enchantments. It is possible that the complete armor would react differently--I can sense its hunger as if it wanted to be whole.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE11_LOG3: I have put on a piece of what I assume to be Graithlan's vessels a dark green metallic armor. Still I cannot find any other remarkable effects aside from its magical enchantments. Maybe only the complete armor would unlock that which so many seem to want or thwart.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE12_LOG0: I have found Hlofor's battle plans in Dasek Moor. A very cunning man this Arctic Bear. He was aware of a search for the Dread Artifacts and sought to step into this affair no doubt wanting to protect his legacy as commander of the Raiders. I can corroborate that his information was correct. I found the item that he was seeking--a green metallic helmet of tangible but dark power. I can assume it is one of Graithlan's vessels mentioned by Hlofor.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE12_LOG1: I have found Hlofor's battle plans in Dasek Moor. A very cunning man this Arctic Bear. His plans include a very interesting map. Fortresses in the Nibenay Basin are marked on the map-- especially Fort Facian and Fort Flecia.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE12_LOG4: I have found Hlofor's battle plans in Dasek Moor. A very cunning man this Arctic Bear. NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE12_LOG5: I have found Hlofor's battle plans in Dasek Moor. A very cunning man this Arctic Bear. NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE13_LOG0: While inspecting Fort Flecia I ran into Faran Hestarius. After a difficult fight I defeated him. He was heir to the Hestarius noble house and next in line for the command of the Raiders. The Arctic Bear had sent him on a mission to find the burial of Graithlan's eldest where one of Graithlan's secrets may lie. His message also mentions Faran's sister Sheil who is to inspect both Fort Entius and Fort Facian. I have already bested Sheil Hestarius. But I did not retrieve Graithlan's relic from Fort Facian. I should return there to get it.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE16_LOG0: When I put on the last piece of the Dread Armor a dark and powerful energy ran through my body. The sensation I felt wearing the incomplete set pales in comparison to what I now experience. The world splits in two the immaterial and the material appear divided and whole at once. I feel as if gliding through the air but my feet are firmly planted on the ground. Somehow joining the armor makes me part of the netherworld. I am now a creature of both worlds. The spectral realm is at my reach.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE17_LOG0: I have discovered Lien Valeth's lair. I read his "legacy" a book that speaks of his designs to achieve otherwordly power through "Graithlan's vessels." He knew of the efforts by other powerful players in acquiring the same power: Eregor--whom I defeated in Yeleri--the Bear which refers to the Raiders' Commander Traven--Master of the Mages Guild--and Molag Bal. Even so Valeth was certain of his ultimate victory. Despite all this he failed. The complete Dread Armor Graithlan's soul is already mine.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE19_LOG0: In Varastal I have fought the Reaver of Ghuul Icxth. This powerful spectral warrior used a unique blade bristling with power from the netherworld. I can wield it safely while wearing my Dread Armor--Graithlan's wraith grants me the power to connect with the spectral realm and thus use such mighty artifacts. Perhaps now that I am the owner of Graithlan's vessels and Icxth's sword I have finally attained the dark power that so many coveted. Yet I am sure that this power will not come without its price eventually.

But the smugglers were right--the legion will not be able to do much given their current situation. Finding the slaver camps would give me further insight into how effective the town's opposition may be.

I wonder what the town's authorities have to say about this.

While exploring inside Collapsed Mine I found a prisoner pen holding several Argonian slaves. This is surprising. Widespread is the news that the authorities of Vvardenfell have banned slavery after the wake of the Nerevarine. If I want more information on them I should inspect these caves and also nearby towns. It is not possible that slave trade has resumed without their rulers finding out. It is just as unlikely as the possibility that these slavers are working alone.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE6_LOG: I have read a logbook from Vandral Lerys the leader of this slaver base. Quite a charmer this Lerys. Other than noting a few Argonian hideouts and names the logbook is not very informative as to how the operation is run. Looks like Lerys expects to conduct it as if it were "business as usual" despite the ban of slavery in Vvandenfell. He does however mention Derahed another leader of Slavers and shipments of slaves from Seran. Rough bunch it would take some fighting to make friends around here.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE7_LOG: I have discovered another group of slavers. They have taken over Nayon Camp--their markings are everywhere. The magnitude of the slavery campaign in these marshes appears at every step even larger than before. Logically its effects must have rippled far and wide--no doubt to nearby towns and to Argonians' ancestral lairs in eastern Cyrodiil. The towns already know about this but it is not clear that they realize how far slave-trade has developed.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE8_LOG: I read Master Lerys' orders to a Slaver contingent at Nayon Camp. From what I read about the Argonian smugglers and Kizra's mission they are fighting back and have attacked the slavers at Seran. Lerys instructs these slavers to raid their way to Seran and then again on their way back to Nayon. I suspect that the Argonians will not yield their strongholds so easily even though the slavers seem well prepared for fighting and are clearly constituted as independent parties with a common tactical organization.

Clearly an Argonian of importance around here given how the small cave is stocked with tools furniture and a bedroll. It does not appear like much but to these frugal Argonians it is a sign of distinction.

NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE12_LO: I have read Yeshani's logbook. It contains a list of important dates and contraband shipments. Its most recent entries are interesting because they chronicle the arrival of the slavers to Black Marsh. Yeshani mentions Eidkee and Kizra of whom I have already learned. Yeshani's wisdom is apparent in these writings--she convinced Eidkee to accept strangers' help should they prove themselves first in exchange for contraband goods because she knew that they cannot rely on the Legion to fight against the slavers.

I have read Yeshani's logbook. It contains a list of important dates and contraband shipments. Its most recent entries are interesting because they chronicle the arrival of the slavers to Blackwood. Yeshani reveals the names and locations of other leaders among these smugglers: Eidkee and Kizra. Yeshani's wisdom is apparent in these writings--she convinced Eidkee to accept strangers' help should they prove themselves first in exchange for contraband goods because they cannot rely on the Legion.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE13_LO: I found a message from Eidkee to Kizra in Red Gill. He asked that a group of smugglers from Kizra's lair attack the slaver encampment at Seran and free its imprisoned Argonian kin. He also wanted Kizra to use her contacts to alert the legion about the slavers' arrival even if he does not trust that they would be able to help much given their current situation. This is why he suggested Kizra to make use of strangers provided that they prove their loyalty. Fighting slavers would be a first step in gaining their trust.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE14_LO: I read Eidkee's inventory log at Blood Clot. The volume of goods moved from this lair is impressive. Eidkee mentions other already familiar names: Yeshani and Kizra. It is clear that they have resorted to solve the problem by themselves. Even though they are proud and secretive this writing further confirms that they now entertain the idea of accepting strangers' help against the slavers. Yet trust will not be easily gained unless loyalty to their cause is proven first--freeing slaved Argonians may be a good start.

I read Eidkee's inventory log at Blood Clot. The volume of goods moved from this lair is impressive. Eidkee mentions other smuggler leaders of the marshes: Yeshani and Kizra. Apparently they do not expect the legion or town's militias to help against the slavers and have resorted to solve the problem by themselves. Even though they are proud and secretive they now entertain the idea of accepting strangers' help against the slavers. Yet trust will not be easily gained unless loyalty to their cause is proven first.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE15_LO: I have opened the prisoner's pen at Seran Camp. The Argonian captives flee towards safety. If I want them to live through this then I must escort them in their escape. The slavers will not take kindly to my action and will try to stop me.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE16_LO: The prisoners of Seran Camp are now safe. My deed will cause a stir in these factions more so since I already freed the captives of Collapsed Mine. The slavers that recognize me will probably try to kill me but the smugglers will regard me with even more respect than before--I may have earned their trust and certainly will be now welcome in both their northern and southern Blackwood hideouts.

The prisoners of Seran Camp are now safe. This deed will not go unnoticed by either faction. The slavers will regard me with suspicion if not outright hostility while the smugglers will more kindly look upon me from now onwards--perhaps I will now be welcome by the Argonians in nearby lairs for they are more likely to know of my actions than those in the northern fringes of the Blackwood.
NewStrings.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE17_LO: I have opened the prisoner's pen at Collapsed Mine. The Argonian captives are taking their chances to escape this deep dungeon. It will be difficult for them to leave unharmed if the slavers try to stop us--with this action I have instantly made enemies of any slaver left in this place. I should escort the fleeing Argonians to safety all the way to their hideout if I want them to live through this.

FullNames.LOC_FN_OOOSylvanRangerQuest: Shadows in a Struggle for Power
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG0: I read an official warning posted in Anvil about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained, the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. What I read in Narind about Eregor and his former ranger brethren shows that their fight is fierce and bound to spill into Anvil itself. Still, I may find more information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG1: I read an official warning posted in Anvil about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained, the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. I have found information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases. Also, I have found a note from Eregor, in Narind, about the nature of this struggle. It is anything but safe outside the walls for the people of the town.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG2: I read an official warning posted in Anvil about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained, the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. Although I have found information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases, there seems to be more than meets the eye. Perhaps I should continue inspecting Gold Coast encampments and Ayleid ruins.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG3: I found the Anvil Fighters Guild report that warned about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer rangers. Apparently, there seems to be two different groups. According to Rhano, they appear engaged in some kind of struggle. He feared the militarized group while the other reacted friendly towards him. Perhaps I should try to ascertain what each group's objectives are. I should be careful though, lest I fall on their wrong side before I can befriend them.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG4: I found the Anvil Fighters Guild report that warned about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer rangers. Rhano has correctly identified two different groups, as well as their opposition. Yet he is not aware of the reason behind this strife. I have already inspected ranger encampments and discovered information regarding Dread Artifacts and the Valenwood Rangers' mission to stop Eregor's search. The plot surrounding the Gold Coast is much more serious than Rhano suspected.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG5: I read a report book in the Anvil Fighters Guild that warned about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer rangers. FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG6: I read an Anvil official's warning about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. My own investigations lead me to believe that the authorities do not yet know the nature of the threat to the whole Gold Coast. Still, they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the vicinity of the town. Aiding the Bosmer Rangers in fighting against Eregor and his armies of corrupted ranger brethren could solve their struggle and end the threat to the people of the Gold Coast.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG7: I read an Anvil official's warning about the sightings of newly-arrived bands of Bosmer Rangers. As I have ascertained, the authorities do not yet know the reason behind this event but they correctly saw it fit to warn their citizens about potential danger in the whereabouts of the town. I may find more information from observing the rangers and inspecting their bases.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG8: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. Azzan seems concerned enough to suggest the Countess to order the posting of warnings around Anvil. Rhano is correct about one of these two groups. Eregor's rangers attacked me on sight. They captured The Storyteller's sacred gem and are searching for 'vessels of Graithlan's soul.' Anvil, and presumably the other ranger faction, would find peace, were I to storm Yeleri and end Eregor and Molag Bal's plans.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG9: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. Azzan seems concerned enough to suggest the Countess to order the posting of warnings around Anvil. Rhano is correct about one of these two groups. I found rangers in the Gold Coast, usually guarding Ayleid ruins, who attacked me on sight. Perhaps the rangers' struggle is connected to the vision I had about the green gem, The Storyteller, and the Ayleid ruin of Narind.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG10: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG11: I found a note from Countess Umbranox's to Dairihill, Anvil Castle's Steward. She is concerned about the report of a Fighters Guild Member called Azzan, which details information about recent and unusual Bosmer ranger sightings in the Gold Coast. It may be worthy to find this report and read its contents--the Countess felt alarmed enough to order the posting of warning signs to Anvil's citizens.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE0_LOG12: I found Azzan's report to Countess Millona Umbranox about recent Bosmer rangers' incursions. Rhano's report suggests that two different groups of rangers are at odds regarding their presence in the Gold Coast. FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE16_LOG0: When I put on the last piece of the Dread Armor a dark and powerful energy ran through my body. The sensation I felt wearing the incomplete set pales in comparison to what I now experience. The world splits in two, the immaterial and the material appear divided and whole at once. I feel as if gliding through the air, but my feet are firmly planted on the ground. Somehow, joining the armor makes me part of the netherworld. I am now a creature of both worlds. The spectral realm is at my reach.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE17_LOG0: I have discovered Lien Valeth's lair. I read his 'legacy,' a book that speaks of his designs to achieve otherwordly power through "Graithlan's vessels." He knew of the efforts by other powerful players in acquiring the same power: Eregor--whom I defeated in Yeleri--the Bear, which refers to the Raiders' Commander, Traven--Master of the Mages Guild--and Molag Bal. Even so, Valeth was certain of his ultimate victory. Despite all this, he failed. The complete Dread Armor, Graithlan's soul, is already mine.

FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSylvanRangerQuest_STAGE19_LOG0: In Varastal, I have fought the Reaver of Ghuul Icxth. This powerful spectral warrior used a unique blade, bristling with power from the netherworld. I can wield it safely while wearing my Dread Armor--Graithlan's wraith grants me the power to connect with the spectral realm and thus use such mighty artifacts. Perhaps, now that I am the owner of Graithlan's vessels and Icxth's sword, I have finally attained the dark power that so many coveted. Yet, I am sure that this power will not come without its price, eventually.

FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE6_LOG: I have read a logbook from Vandral Lerys, the leader of this slaver base. Quite a charmer, this Lerys. He does, however, mention Derahed, another leader of Slavers and shipments of slaves from Seran. Rough bunch, it would take some fighting to make friends around here.

FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE8_LOG: I read Master Lerys' orders to a Slaver contingent at Nayon Camp. I suspect that the Argonians will not yield their strongholds so easily, even though the slavers seem well prepared for fighting and are clearly constituted as independent parties with a common tactical organization.

FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE12_LO: I have read Yeshani's logbook. It contains a list of important dates and contraband shipments. Its most recent entries are interesting because they chronicle the arrival of the slavers to Black Marsh. Yeshani mentions Eidkee and Kizra, of whom I have already learned. Yeshani's wisdom is apparent in these writings--she convinced Eidkee to accept strangers' help, should they prove themselves first, in exchange for contraband goods, because she knew that they cannot rely on the Legion to fight against the slavers.

I have read Yeshani's logbook. It contains a list of important dates and contraband shipments. Its most recent entries are interesting because they chronicle the arrival of the slavers to Blackwood. Yeshani reveals the names and locations of other leaders among these smugglers: Eidkee and Kizra. Yeshani's wisdom is apparent in these writings--she convinced Eidkee to accept strangers' help, should they prove themselves first, in exchange for contraband goods, because they cannot rely on the Legion.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE13_LO: I found a message from Eidkee to Kizra in Red Gill. He asked that a group of smugglers from Kizra's lair attack the slaver encampment at Seran and free its imprisoned Argonian kin. He also wanted Kizra to use her contacts to alert the legion about the slavers' arrival, even if he does not trust that they would be able to help much, given their current situation. This is why he suggested Kizra to make use of strangers provided that they prove their loyalty. Fighting slavers would be a first step in gaining their trust.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE14_LO: I read Eidkee's inventory log at Blood Clot. The volume of goods moved from this lair is impressive. Eidkee mentions other already familiar names: Yeshani and Kizra. It is clear that they have resorted to solve the problem by themselves. Even though they are proud and secretive, this writing further confirms that they now entertain the idea of accepting strangers' help against the slavers. Yet, trust will not be easily gained unless loyalty to their cause is proven first--freeing slaved Argonians may be a good start.

I read Eidkee's inventory log at Blood Clot. The volume of goods moved from this lair is impressive. Eidkee mentions other smuggler leaders of the marshes: Yeshani and Kizra. Apparently they do not expect the legion or town's militias to help against the slavers and have resorted to solve the problem by themselves. Even though they are proud and secretive, they now entertain the idea of accepting strangers' help against the slavers. Yet, trust will not be easily gained unless loyalty to their cause is proven first.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE15_LO: I have opened the prisoner's pen at Seran Camp. The Argonian captives flee towards safety. If I want them to live through this, then I must escort them in their escape. The slavers will not take kindly to my action and will try to stop me.
FullNames.LOC_LE_OOOSlaversAndSmugglers_STAGE16_LO: The prisoners of Seran Camp are now safe. My deed will cause a stir in these factions, more so since I already freed the captives of Collapsed Mine. The slavers that recognize me will probably try to kill me but the smugglers will regard me with even more respect than before--I may have earned their trust, and certainly will be now welcome in both their northern and southern Blackwood hideouts.

The prisoners of Seran Camp are now safe. This deed will not go unnoticed by either faction. The slavers will regard me with suspicion, if not outright hostility, while the smugglers will more kindly look upon me from now onwards--perhaps I will now be welcome by the Argonians in nearby lairs, for they are more likely to know of my actions than those in the northern fringes of the Blackwood.

I should escort the fleeing Argonians to safety, all the way to their hideout, if I want them to live through this.
